title: Manali Traffic Video: Bumper-to-Bumper Traffic Seen in Himachal Pradesh City As Tourists Flock to Hills To Beat the Heat; Clip Surfaces - LatestLY
full_text:
Manali Traffic Video: Bumper-to-Bumper Traffic Seen in Himachal Pradesh City As Tourists Flock to Hills To Beat the Heat; Clip Surfaces
A massive weekend influx of tourists escaping 43 degrees Celsius plus heatwaves in the plains has caused severe traffic gridlock in Manali. National Highway 3 and roads to the Atal Tunnel face bumper-to-bumper congestion. Local police have deployed extra personnel and issued travel advisories urging motorists to use lane discipline and travel early.
A massive influx of tourists seeking relief from intense heatwaves across the plains has triggered severe traffic congestion and slowed vehicular movement in and around Himachal Pradesh's Manali. Local authorities reported that thousands of vehicles have entered the popular hill station over the weekend, stretching the region's traffic infrastructure to its limits and causing hours-long delays for commuters on major arterial routes.
Severe Gridlocks Across Key Corridors
The surge in tourist arrivals has led to significant bottlenecks along the crucial National Highway 3 (NH3) and the prominent roads leading toward the Atal Tunnel and Solang Valley. According to local traffic monitoring updates, vehicles were seen moving at a crawl, with bumper-to-bumper congestion extending for several kilometers along the entry points to the town. Weather Forecast Today, May 24, 2026: Check Weather Updates, Rainfall and Heatwave Predictions for Mumbai, Delhi, Chennai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata and Shimla.
Slow Vehicular Movement Seen in Manali As Tourists Escape to the Hills
#WATCH | Himachal Pradesh: Traffic congestion and slow vehicular movement witnessed in Manali as people throng to hilly areas to get some respite from the scorching heat. pic.twitter.com/8SL6MwtTXK
— ANI (@ANI) May 24, 2026
A video shared by news agency ANI showed traffic congestion and slow vehicular movement in Manali as people thronged to hilly areas to get some respite from the scorching heat. Local police personnel have been deployed in increased numbers at major intersections, including the Green Tax barrier and the Mall Road junctions, to manage the overwhelming volume of incoming cars and luxury tourist buses. Officials noted that the high volume of single-occupancy personal vehicles from neighboring states has significantly added to the gridlock.
Manali Live Weather Forecast and Updates
Plains Heatwave Drives Tourist Influx
The sudden spike in travel comes as temperatures across neighboring plains - including Delhi, Punjab, Haryana, and Chandigarh - continue to hover well above 43 degrees Celsius. The extreme summer conditions have prompted a weekend exodus toward higher altitudes, where temperatures remain comparatively pleasant at around 20 to 25 degrees Celsius. Hotel operators in Manali reported a sharp rise in occupancy rates, with weekend bookings nearing full capacity. While the economic boost has been welcomed by local business owners, the simultaneous arrival of thousands of travelers has heavily burdened local civic amenities and parking spaces. Administrative Response and Advisory for Travelers
In response to the persistent gridlock, the Kullu district administration and local police have issued travel guidelines to ease the bottleneck. Authorities are urging motorists to strictly adhere to designated lane discipline and avoid overtaking on narrow hill stretches, which frequently causes prolonged blockades. Travelers planning to visit Solang Valley, Rohtang Pass, or the Atal Tunnel are being advised to start their journeys early in the morning to avoid peak congestion hours. Local law enforcement is also utilizing restricted one-way traffic routing in high-density zones within Manali town to maintain a steady, albeit slow, flow of vehicles.
(The above story first appeared on LatestLY on May 24, 2026 06:33 PM IST. For more news and updates on politics, world, sports, entertainment and lifestyle, log on to our website latestly.com).

मैदानी इलाकों में बढ़ती गर्मी से राहत पाने के लिए पर्यटक हिमाचल प्रदेश की वादियों का रूख कर रहे हैं। खासकर कुल्लू-मनाली में पर्यटकों की आमद बढ़ गई है। यहां पहुंचकर पर्यटक रोहतांग क्षेत्र में बर्फ के दीदार करने को प्राथमिकता दे रहे हैं। मढ़ी से रोहतांग तक पर्यटक बर्फ की परत पर कदमताल कर रहे हैं। यहां एक दूसरे पर बर्फ के गोले बरसाते हुए भी पर्यटकों को देखा जा सकता है। जबकि बर्फीली वादी में पर्यटक बर्फ के साथ की जा रही अठखेलियों को कैमरे और अपने मोबाइल में भी कैद कर रहे हैं।
